Non-obvious fail-safe contract that a reader would get wrong."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/benchmark/extended_metrics.py","symbol":"faithfulness_precision","line":76,"category":"SAFETY GATE","why":"Core faithfulness metric — verified material atoms / material atoms; the material-only filtering and None-on-empty-denominator semantics are load-bearing and undocumented."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/benchmark/extended_metrics.py","symbol":"citation_support_rate","line":86,"category":"SAFETY GATE","why":"Verified-AND-resolved-citation over material atoms; distinct from faithfulness_precision (adds citation resolution requirement). The distinction is non-obvious."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/benchmark/extended_metrics.py","symbol":"required_entity_recall","line":118,"category":"SAFETY GATE","why":"Rubric coverage recall with pending=True sentinel when no rubric — the pending vs zero distinction is a fail-safe that must not read as a false 0."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/audit_ir/contract_draft_store.py","symbol":"reject_draft","line":1082,"category":"SAFETY/DELETION GATE + STATE TRANSITION","why":"Contract-draft rejection state transition; enforces LAW II (non-empty rationale for the SOC2 audit trail) and raises ContractDraftStateError. Safety-gated state mutation."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/audit_ir/job_queue.py","symbol":"mark_completed","line":416,"category":"STATE TRANSITION","why":"Guarded running->completed transition: conditional UPDATE only fires WHERE status='running', raises JobQueueError on unknown job or wrong-state. The guard semantics (rowcount==0 disambiguation) are non-obvious."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/audit_ir/job_queue.py","symbol":"mark_failed","line":457,"category":"STATE TRANSITION","why":"Guarded running->failed transition mirroring mark_completed; same rowcount-based state guard and error contract."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/nodes/scope_gate.py","symbol":"search","line":531,"category":"NON-OBVIOUS GATE HELPER","why":"Intervention-recognizer scan: returns first regex match that passes min-token-length AND exclude-word filters — the filtering that distinguishes it from a raw re.search is the whole point and is undocumented."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/followup/refusal.py","symbol":"detect_out_of_scope","line":32,"category":"SAFETY/REFUSAL GATE","why":"Out-of-scope refusal gate for follow-ups: keyword-overlap threshold against parent template, with 'general' template short-circuit. Decides refusal — safety-relevant and non-obvious."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/nodes/corpus_adequacy_gate.py","symbol":"CorpusAdequacyReport","line":210,"category":"STATE/GATE DTO","why":"Adequacy gate verdict object exposing BOTH raw-pool and on-topic denominators so Methods cannot claim '8/8 grounded' over a contaminated pool. The dual-denominator disclosure invariant is subtle and load-bearing."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/nodes/corpus_adequacy_gate.py","symbol":"AdequacyFinding","line":201,"category":"GATE DTO","why":"Per-check adequacy finding with severity in {ok,warn,critical}; the severity ladder drives gate decisions and the enum values are only in a trailing comment."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/adequacy/plan_sufficiency_gate.py","symbol":"PlanSufficiencyReport","line":110,"category":"GATE DTO","why":"Plan-sufficiency gate verdict carrying authority_floor, per-facet minimums, round/max_rounds, and under-covered units — the fields that decide whether another retrieval round fires."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/retrieval/winner_firing_gate.py","symbol":"WinnerFiringVerdict","line":111,"category":"GATE DTO","why":"Winner-firing gate verdict; abort=True means the run MUST stop before generation. winners_checked is a disclosure manifest, never a drop list — a distinction a reader would miss."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/retrieval/quality_eligibility.py","symbol":"EligibilityPlan","line":82,"category":"STATE/ELIGIBILITY DTO","why":"Post-fetch eligibility outcome: excluded-ids MASK the citable menu (rows KEPT in corpus+disclosure), a demote-weight map for SOFT order-not-drop preferences, plus receipts. The keep-but-mask vs drop semantics are non-obvious."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/planning/planning_gate_schema.py","symbol":"plan_from_dict","line":1256,"category":"STATE DESERIALIZER","why":"Top-level ResearchExecutionPlan deserializer — the state schema entrypoint. Validates dict shape, defaults plan_version, and recursively builds every sub-structure while silently dropping non-dict entries."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/planning/eligibility_judge.py","symbol":"is_empty","line":377,"category":"STATE PREDICATE","why":"EligibilityPlan.is_empty short-circuit — deliberately ignores excluded_records (only excluded_ids/weights/receipts count as non-empty). The asymmetry is a real seam-inertness contract, easy to misread."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/audit_ir/loader.py","symbol":"get_bibliography_by_evidence_id","line":441,"category":"NON-OBVIOUS AUDIT ACCESSOR","why":"Maps an evidence id back to its bibliography entry for audit-IR provenance; the id->source resolution is part of the evidence traceability contract."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/audit_ir/loader.py","symbol":"get_contradictions_for_evidence","line":449,"category":"NON-OBVIOUS AUDIT ACCESSOR","why":"Surfaces recorded contradictions for a given evidence id — feeds the contradiction-disclosure path; not a trivial getter (cross-references contradiction store)."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/audit_ir/loader.py","symbol":"get_evidence_spans_for_claim","line":480,"category":"NON-OBVIOUS AUDIT ACCESSOR","why":"Claim->evidence-span resolution underpinning span-level faithfulness verification; the claim-to-span join is the traceability primitive."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/audit_ir/loader.py","symbol":"get_tier_counts","line":466,"category":"NON-OBVIOUS AUDIT ACCESSOR","why":"Source-tier histogram used by tier-compliance gates; the tier taxonomy meaning is non-obvious to a reader."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/anti_sycophancy/stance_delta.py","symbol":"compute_stance_delta","line":59,"category":"SAFETY/ANTI-SYCOPHANCY","why":"Computes framing-stance shift between input and output to detect sycophantic drift; the delta definition drives an anti-sycophancy safety signal."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/anti_sycophancy/stance_delta.py","symbol":"StanceDeltaReport","line":36,"category":"SAFETY DTO","why":"Anti-sycophancy verdict object; fields encode the stance-drift signal consumed downstream."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/authority/junk_detection.py","symbol":"JunkResult","line":22,"category":"GATE DTO","why":"Junk/low-authority detection verdict feeding source-eligibility filtering; the fields determine whether a source is dropped."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/auto_induction/precision_metrics.py","symbol":"passes_acceptance","line":172,"category":"SAFETY/ACCEPTANCE GATE","why":"Acceptance gate for auto-induced keywords — the precision/coverage thresholds it enforces decide whether an induced ruleset is admitted."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/tools/openalex_client.py","symbol":"authority_tier","line":62,"category":"NON-OBVIOUS TIER MAPPING","why":"Maps an OpenAlex source to a POLARIS authority tier; the tier assignment logic feeds every tier-compliance gate and is non-obvious."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/wiki/mesh/retrieve/lethal.py","symbol":"RetrievalResult","line":64,"category":"STATE DTO","why":"Result object for the 'lethal' retrieval path; 26-line dataclass whose fields carry the retrieval provenance consumed by compose. Non-trivial shape."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/wiki/mesh/compose/composer.py","symbol":"ComposeResult","line":55,"category":"STATE DTO","why":"Composition output object carrying the composed claim graph + provenance; downstream serialization depends on its field contract."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/wiki/mesh/store.py","symbol":"insert_claim","line":356,"category":"STATE TRANSITION","why":"57-line claim-insertion mutation into the mesh store; establishes claim identity + edges. The persistence/identity contract is non-obvious."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/wiki/mesh/store.py","symbol":"workspace_stats","line":859,"category":"NON-OBVIOUS AGGREGATOR","why":"Aggregates per-workspace mesh statistics (claims/questions/answers/ops); the counted entities and their meaning aid reviewers."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/evaluator/live_judge.py","symbol":"LiveJudgeResult","line":64,"category":"GATE DTO","why":"Live-judge verdict object; the fields carry the pass/fail signal and rationale used to gate outputs."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/audit_ir/scope_classifier_llm.py","symbol":"classify","line":179,"category":"NON-OBVIOUS CLASSIFIER","why":"LLM scope classifier entrypoint (74 lines) deciding in/out-of-scope routing; the classification contract and fallback behavior are load-bearing for scope enforcement."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/synthesis/source_necessity.py","symbol":"bfs","line":73,"category":"NON-OBVIOUS ALGORITHM","why":"BFS over the claim-support graph to determine minimal necessary source set; the traversal + termination semantics are non-obvious and drive source-necessity pruning."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/planning/contract_compliance.py","symbol":"counts","line":122,"category":"GATE AGGREGATOR","why":"Compliance-count aggregation feeding the contract-compliance gate verdict; the tallied categories are non-obvious from the signature."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/generator/span_quality_gate.py","symbol":"primary_model","line":null,"category":"SAFETY-GATE CONFIG","why":"Resolves the primary model used by the span-quality gate (env-driven); non-obvious because it selects the judge model that gates generated spans."},{"file":"src/polaris_graph/generator/span_quality_gate.py","symbol":"escalation_model","line":null,"category":"SAFETY-GATE CONFIG","why":"Resolves the escalation model the span-quality gate falls back to on disagreement; the escalation semantics (when it fires) are the whole point."}]. Docstrings only, no logic. Accurate Raises/return (verify vs code). Skip load-bearing __doc__ (getdoc/help/doctest/fastapi/pydantic). For safety gates + state transitions, document the CONTRACT (what invariant it enforces, what it raises/returns). Keep collection at 16738 tests collected, 11 errors (via: /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python -m pytest tests/ --collect-only -q | tail -1, run in /home/polaris/wt/phase3ab4 AFTER copying the phase0 oracle harness). Note: pre-harness collection was 16726 tests / 11 errors; the oracle harness adds 12 collected tests. The 11 errors are pre-existing on branch chore/review-readiness-phase3a-b3 and unrelated to this batch.; py_compile. Return docstrings_added, files_changed, skipped_loadbearing, collection_after.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

60	    """One audited claim paired with its prose text (text is used ONLY for dedup —
61	    never for verdict assignment, which already happened upstream)."""
62	    text: str
63	    row: ClaimRow
64	
65	
66	def _is_material(row: ClaimRow) -> bool:
67	    return row.severity in _MATERIAL_SEVERITIES
68	
69	
70	def _has_resolved_citation(row: ClaimRow) -> bool:
71	    """A claim is citation-supported only if it carries a non-empty resolved
72	    citation id (an unresolved / None citation does NOT count — traceability floor)."""
73	    return bool(row.citation_id)
74	
75	
76	def faithfulness_precision(rows: list[ClaimRow]) -> dict:
77	    material = [r for r in rows if _is_material(r)]
78	    denom = len(material)
79	    verified = sum(1 for r in material if r.verdict == "VERIFIED")
80	    return {
81	        "material_atoms": denom,
82	        "verified": verified,
83	        "value": (verified / denom) if denom else None,
84	    }
85	
86	
87	def citation_support_rate(rows: list[ClaimRow]) -> dict:
88	    material = [r for r in rows if _is_material(r)]
89	    denom = len(material)
90	    supported = sum(
91	        1 for r in material if r.verdict == "VERIFIED" and _has_resolved_citation(r)
92	    )
93	    return {
94	        "material_atoms": denom,
95	        "verified_and_cited": supported,
96	        "value": (supported / denom) if denom else None,
97	    }
98	
99	
100	def diversity_score(rows: list[ClaimRow]) -> dict:
101	    verified = [r for r in rows if r.verdict == "VERIFIED"]
102	    denom = len(verified)
103	    distinct_sources = {r.citation_id for r in verified if r.citation_id}
104	    return {
105	        "verified_claims": denom,
106	        "distinct_sources": len(distinct_sources),
107	        "value": (len(distinct_sources) / denom) if denom else None,

112	def required_entity_recall(rubric: list[RubricElement] | None) -> dict:
113	    if not rubric:
114	        return {"total_required": 0, "covered_supported": 0, "value": None,
115	                "pending": True}
116	    total = len(rubric)
117	    covered_supported = sum(1 for e in rubric if e.covered and e.citation_supported)
118	    return {
119	        "total_required": total,
120	        "covered_supported": covered_supported,
121	        "value": (covered_supported / total) if total else None,
122	        "missing": [e.element_id for e in rubric
123	                    if not (e.covered and e.citation_supported)],
124	        "pending": False,
125	    }
126	
127	
128	def safety_floor_recall(
129	    rubric: list[RubricElement] | None, safety_element_ids: set[str] | None,
130	) -> dict:
131	    if not rubric or not safety_element_ids:
132	        return {"total_safety_required": 0, "covered_supported": 0, "value": None,
133	                "pending": True}
134	    # Denominator = the PRE-REGISTERED tagged count, NOT the count present in the
135	    # supplied rubric (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P2): a tagged id missing from the rubric
136	    # must SURFACE + count AGAINST recall (fail-safe), never silently shrink the
137	    # denominator into a falsely-high recall.
138	    rubric_by_id = {e.element_id: e for e in rubric}
139	    total = len(safety_element_ids)
140	    covered_supported = 0
141	    missing: list[str] = []

143	    for eid in sorted(safety_element_ids):
144	        e = rubric_by_id.get(eid)
145	        if e is None:
146	            missing_from_rubric.append(eid)
147	            missing.append(eid)
148	            continue
149	        if e.covered and e.citation_supported:
150	            covered_supported += 1
151	        else:
152	            missing.append(eid)
153	    return {
154	        "total_safety_required": total,
155	        "covered_supported": covered_supported,
156	        "value": (covered_supported / total) if total else None,
157	        "missing": missing,
158	        "missing_from_rubric": missing_from_rubric,
159	        "pending": False,
160	    }

406	                    f"resume_paused: job {job_id} status={job.status} is not paused"
407	                )
408	        return self._must_get(job_id)
409	
410	    def mark_paused(self, job_id: str) -> Job:
411	        """Worker calls this when it hits a checkpoint after pause was requested."""
412	        self._transition(job_id, "running", "paused", paused_at=time.time(),
413	                         pause_requested=0)
414	        return self._must_get(job_id)
415	
416	    def mark_completed(self, job_id: str, artifact_dir: str | None = None) -> Job:
417	        with self._connect() as conn:
418	            now = time.time()
419	            cursor = conn.execute(
420	                "UPDATE jobs SET status='completed', completed_at=?, artifact_dir=?, "
421	                "progress_pct=100.0, progress_message='completed' "
422	                "WHERE job_id=? AND status='running'",
423	                (now, artifact_dir, job_id),
424	            )
425	            if cursor.rowcount == 0:
426	                job = self.get(job_id)
427	                if job is None:
428	                    raise JobQueueError(f"mark_completed: unknown job {job_id}")
429	                raise JobQueueError(
430	                    f"mark_completed: job {job_id} status={job.status} not running"
431	                )
432	        return self._must_get(job_id)
433	
434	    def mark_cancelled(self, job_id: str) -> Job:
435	        """Worker calls this after honoring a cancel request mid-run.
436	
437	        Pending/paused jobs are cancelled atomically via request_cancel()
438	        (no live worker needed). This path only fires when a worker
439	        catches JobControl.Cancelled mid-run.
440	        """
441	        with self._connect() as conn:
442	            now = time.time()
443	            cursor = conn.execute(
444	                "UPDATE jobs SET status='cancelled', completed_at=? "
445	                "WHERE job_id=? AND status='running'",
446	                (now, job_id),
447	            )
448	            if cursor.rowcount == 0:
449	                job = self.get(job_id)
450	                if job is None:
451	                    raise JobQueueError(f"mark_cancelled: unknown job {job_id}")
452	                raise JobQueueError(
453	                    f"mark_cancelled: job {job_id} status={job.status} not running"
454	                )
455	        return self._must_get(job_id)
456	
457	    def mark_failed(self, job_id: str, error: str) -> Job:
458	        with self._connect() as conn:
459	            now = time.time()
460	            cursor = conn.execute(
461	                "UPDATE jobs SET status='failed', completed_at=?, error=? "
462	                "WHERE job_id=? AND status='running'",
463	                (now, error, job_id),
464	            )
465	            if cursor.rowcount == 0:
466	                job = self.get(job_id)
467	                if job is None:
468	                    raise JobQueueError(f"mark_failed: unknown job {job_id}")
469	                raise JobQueueError(
470	                    f"mark_failed: job {job_id} status={job.status} not running"
471	                )
472	        return self._must_get(job_id)
473	
474	    def record_progress(
475	        self,
476	        job_id: str,
477	        progress_pct: float,
478	        progress_message: str = "",
479	        checkpoint: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
480	    ) -> Job:
481	        """Worker calls this to update progress and persist checkpoint state.
482	
483	        Pre-condition: job is running. Raises if not.
484	        """
485	        if not (0.0 <= progress_pct <= 100.0):
486	            raise JobQueueError(f"record_progress: progress_pct {progress_pct} out of [0,100]")
487	        with self._connect() as conn:
488	            cursor = conn.execute(
489	                "UPDATE jobs SET progress_pct=?, progress_message=?, checkpoint_json=? "
490	                "WHERE job_id=? AND status='running'",
491	                (
492	                    float(progress_pct),
493	                    str(progress_message),
494	                    json.dumps(dict(checkpoint)) if checkpoint is not None else None,
495	                    job_id,
496	                ),
497	            )
498	            if cursor.rowcount == 0:
499	                job = self.get(job_id)
500	                if job is None:
501	                    raise JobQueueError(f"record_progress: unknown job {job_id}")
502	                raise JobQueueError(
503	                    f"record_progress: job {job_id} status={job.status} not running"
504	                )
505	        return self._must_get(job_id)
506	
507	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
508	    # Internals
509	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

1	"""I-perm-024 (#1216) — beat-both scorer metric extension (claim-by-claim only).
2	
3	Five extended metrics for the DR head-to-head, computed STRICTLY from the
4	already-audited per-claim ledger (``ClaimRow``s — each a reconciled VERIFIED/
5	PARTIAL/UNSUPPORTED/FABRICATED/UNREACHABLE verdict against the FETCHED cited span)
6	and the FROZEN gold rubric (``RubricElement``s whose ``covered`` /
7	``citation_supported`` flags were set by the SAME dual §-1.1 audit).
8	
9	§-1.1 STRUCTURAL GUARANTEE (the load-bearing safety property): every function here
10	accepts ONLY ``ClaimRow`` / ``RubricElement`` typed inputs. None of them receives or
11	opens raw report text. So they CANNOT do string-presence / pattern-presence matching
12	("does the report mention 'tirzepatide'?") — that is structurally impossible, not a
13	convention. Each metric is a roll-up over the per-claim audit LEDGER, never over raw
14	text. This is what keeps ``required_entity_recall`` / ``diversity_score`` off the
15	§-1.1 BANNED list (word/citation/source COUNTS as quality, pattern-presence,
16	string-presence PASS/FAIL — "lethal in clinical").
17	
18	The metrics:
19	  1. ``faithfulness_precision`` = VERIFIED / material(S0-S2).
20	  2. ``citation_support_rate`` = VERIFIED-with-a-RESOLVED-citation / material.
21	  3. ``required_entity_recall`` = covered+citation_supported frozen rubric elements
22	     / total (the existing ``lane2_coverage`` semantics, surfaced as recall).
23	  4. ``safety_floor_recall`` = #3 restricted to the PRE-REGISTERED safety element
24	     subset (``safety_floor_elements_v3.json``, pinned to the rubric sha).
25	  5. ``diversity_score`` = distinct resolved sources among VERIFIED claims / VERIFIED
26	     count — DIAGNOSTIC ONLY (support-concentration), NOT a superiority signal.
27	
28	Claimify-style dedup (``claim_dedup``) collapses near-duplicate claim atoms BEFORE
29	#1/#2/#5 so a verbose report cannot inflate by restating one easy fact. Applied
30	identically to every system.
31	"""
32	from __future__ import annotations
33	
34	import json
35	from dataclasses import dataclass
36	from pathlib import Path
37	
38	from src.polaris_graph.benchmark.claim_audit_scorer import ClaimRow, RubricElement
39	from src.polaris_graph.benchmark.claim_dedup import dedup_claims
40	
41	_MATERIAL_SEVERITIES = ("S0", "S1", "S2")
42	
43	# Default location of the pre-registered safety-floor element registry. Lives under
44	# tracked config/ (NOT runtime outputs/, which is gitignored) — it is hand-authored
45	# pre-registration, committed, not a regenerated artifact.
46	_SAFETY_FLOOR_REGISTRY = (
47	    Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]
48	    / "config" / "dr_benchmark" / "safety_floor_elements_v3.json"
49	)
50	
51	_DIVERSITY_NOTE = (
52	    "diversity_score is a support-concentration DIAGNOSTIC (distinct sources among "
53	    "VERIFIED claims / VERIFIED count), NOT a superiority signal; a higher value is "
54	    "NOT a §-1.1 'win' (unique-source counts are banned as quality signals)."
55	)
56	
57	
58	@dataclass(frozen=True)
59	class ScoredClaim:
60	    """One audited claim paired with its prose text (text is used ONLY for dedup —

515	    matches. The denylist removes residual common-English collisions that the
516	    stem anchoring + min-length cannot separate (e.g. accept/except/concept/
517	    intercept for the `-cept` fusion-protein stem). Exposes the subset of the
518	    `re.Pattern` API the recognizer uses (`search`).
519	    """
520	
521	    def __init__(
522	        self,
523	        pattern: re.Pattern[str],
524	        min_token_len: int,
525	        exclude_words: frozenset[str],
526	    ) -> None:
527	        self._pattern = pattern
528	        self._min_token_len = min_token_len
529	        self._exclude_words = exclude_words
530	
531	    def search(self, text: str) -> Optional[re.Match[str]]:
532	        for m in self._pattern.finditer(text):
533	            token = m.group(0)
534	            if len(token) < self._min_token_len:
535	                continue
536	            if token.lower() in self._exclude_words:
537	                continue
538	            return m
539	        return None
540	
541	
542	# Module-level cache: the config is read once per process (deterministic, no I/O
543	# on the hot path after first use). Reset in tests via _reset_intervention_cache.
544	_intervention_recognizer_cache: Optional[_InterventionRecognizer] = None
545	
546	
547	def _get_intervention_recognizer() -> _InterventionRecognizer:
548	    global _intervention_recognizer_cache
549	    if _intervention_recognizer_cache is None:
550	        _intervention_recognizer_cache = _build_intervention_recognizer(
551	            _INTERVENTION_RECOGNITION_CONFIG
552	        )
553	    return _intervention_recognizer_cache
554	
555	
556	def _reset_intervention_cache() -> None:
557	    """Test hook: clear the compiled-recognizer cache."""
558	    global _intervention_recognizer_cache
559	    _intervention_recognizer_cache = None

1	"""Out-of-scope refusal handling for follow-up runs (I-f11-004).
2	
3	Heuristic: a follow-up is out-of-scope when zero of its tokens
4	(case-insensitive, punctuation-stripped) appear in the parent
5	template's `_`-separated keyword list. MVP debt: single-token templates
6	over-refuse; LLM-augmented intent matching is post-MVP.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import re
12	from dataclasses import dataclass
13	
14	from polaris_graph.followup.agent import (
15	    ComposedQuery,
16	    FollowUpAgent,
17	    ParentRunContext,
18	)
19	
20	
21	_PUNCT_RE = re.compile(r"[^a-z0-9 ]+")
22	
23	
24	@dataclass(frozen=True)
25	class RefusalDecision:
26	    is_refused: bool
27	    reason: str | None
28	    template_keywords: list[str]
29	    question_overlap: list[str]
30	
31	
32	def detect_out_of_scope(
33	    parent_template: str, follow_up: str, *, min_overlap: int = 1
34	) -> RefusalDecision:
35	    keywords = [t for t in parent_template.lower().split("_") if t]
36	    if parent_template == "general":
37	        return RefusalDecision(False, None, keywords, [])
38	    words = _PUNCT_RE.sub(" ", follow_up.lower()).split()
39	    kw_set = set(keywords)
40	    overlap = [w for w in words if w in kw_set]
41	    if len(overlap) < min_overlap:
42	        reason = (
43	            f"follow-up has {len(overlap)} shared keyword(s) with parent "
44	            f"template '{parent_template}' (need >= {min_overlap}); out-of-scope"
45	        )
46	        return RefusalDecision(True, reason, keywords, overlap)
47	    return RefusalDecision(False, None, keywords, overlap)
48	
49	
50	def compose_or_refuse(
51	    agent: FollowUpAgent, parent: ParentRunContext, follow_up: str
52	) -> ComposedQuery | RefusalDecision:
53	    decision = detect_out_of_scope(parent.template, follow_up)
54	    if decision.is_refused:
55	        return decision
56	    return agent.compose(parent, follow_up)
57

1256	def plan_from_dict(d: Any) -> ResearchExecutionPlan:
1257	    if not isinstance(d, dict):
1258	        raise SchemaError(f"plan must be an object, got {type(d).__name__}")
1259	    return ResearchExecutionPlan(
1260	        plan_version=_as_opt_str(d.get("plan_version")) or SCHEMA_VERSION,
1261	        threads=[ResearchThread.from_dict(t) for t in (d.get("threads") or []) if isinstance(t, dict)],
1262	        evidence_needs=[EvidenceNeed.from_dict(e) for e in (d.get("evidence_needs") or []) if isinstance(e, dict)],
1263	        query_intents=[QueryIntent.from_dict(q) for q in (d.get("query_intents") or []) if isinstance(q, dict)],
1264	        outline_seed=[OutlineSeed.from_dict(o) for o in (d.get("outline_seed") or []) if isinstance(o, dict)],
1265	        coverage_matrix=[CoverageBinding.from_dict(c) for c in (d.get("coverage_matrix") or []) if isinstance(c, dict)],
1266	        budget=BudgetEnvelope.from_dict(d.get("budget") or {}),
1267	        stop_conditions=_as_str_list(d.get("stop_conditions")),
1268	    )

1276	class ClarificationQuestion:
1277	    """A question posed to the user to resolve an ambiguity, with choices and an optional answer.
1278	
1279	    ``to_dict`` serializes to a plain dict.
1280	    """

1282	    question_id: str
1283	    prompt: str
1284	    choices: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)

1287	    answer: Optional[str] = None

1291	            "question_id": self.question_id,
1292	            "prompt": self.prompt,
1293	            "choices": list(self.choices),
1294	            "affected_term_ids": list(self.affected_term_ids),
1295	            "why_it_matters": self.why_it_matters,
1296	            "answer": self.answer,
1297	        }

435	    def get_bibliography_by_num(self, num: int) -> BibliographyEntry | None:
436	        for entry in self.bibliography:
437	            if entry.num == num:
438	                return entry
439	        return None
440	
441	    def get_bibliography_by_evidence_id(
442	        self, evidence_id: str
443	    ) -> BibliographyEntry | None:
444	        for entry in self.bibliography:
445	            if entry.evidence_id == evidence_id:
446	                return entry
447	        return None
448	
449	    def get_contradictions_for_evidence(
450	        self, evidence_id: str
451	    ) -> tuple[ContradictionCluster, ...]:
452	        out = []
453	        for cluster in self.contradictions:
454	            if any(c.evidence_id == evidence_id for c in cluster.claims):
455	                out.append(cluster)
456	        return tuple(out)
457	
458	    def get_frame_coverage_for_entity(
459	        self, entity_id: str
460	    ) -> FrameCoverageEntry | None:
461	        for entry in self.frame_coverage.entries:
462	            if entry.entity_id == entity_id:
463	                return entry
464	        return None
465	
466	    def get_tier_counts(self) -> dict[str, int]:
467	        return {
468	            tier: int(round(frac * self.tier_mix.corpus_count))
469	            for tier, frac in self.tier_mix.fractions.items()
470	        }
471	
472	    def get_sentence_by_claim_id(self, claim_id: str) -> ReportSentence | None:
473	        """Foundation lookup for Evidence Inspector view 1 (Report click-to-inspect)."""
474	        for section in self.verified_report.sections:
475	            for sentence in section.sentences:
476	                if sentence.claim_id == claim_id:
477	                    return sentence
478	        return None
479	
480	    def get_evidence_spans_for_claim(
481	        self, claim_id: str
482	    ) -> tuple[EvidenceSpanToken, ...]:
483	        sentence = self.get_sentence_by_claim_id(claim_id)
484	        return sentence.tokens if sentence is not None else ()
